title: draba cinerea subsp. groenlandica
intro: Draba cinerea subsp. groenlandica, also known as the Greenland whitlow-grass, is a rare and endangered plant species that belongs to the family Brassicaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be used to treat various health issues such as wounds and skin infections. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable resource for treating various health conditions. In herbal preparations, Draba cinerea subsp. groenlandica is often used in the form of infusions or tinctures, and its leaves and flowers can be used to create topical applications for wound care and skin health.
